sections.title: 第 74 页
sections.text:
Unit 7
How do we celebrate festivals?

sections.title: 第 75 页
sections.text:

B Think and write.

sections.title: 第 76 页
sections.text:
**How do Li Bo and Wu Yan spend the Spring Festival? Read and write the differences.**

**Li Bo:**
- **Location:** Guangzhou
- **Food:** Rice cakes
- **Activity:** Visiting the flower market

**Wu Yan:**
- **Location:** Beijing
- **Food:** Jiaozi (dumplings)
- **Activity:** Doing paper-cutting and watching lion dances

**Answer the questions before you read.**
1. Which festival do you think is the biggest for Chinese people?
2. What do people do at this festival?

sections.title: 第 77 页
sections.text:
Explore Important festivals 新春大吉

To Katie
Subject: Spring Festival

Dear Katie,
I'm very excited! It'll be the Spring Festival next month. We'll clean our house. This is hard work, but it'll bring good luck. I'll help my grandmother make jiaozi; it's my favorite food. Then I'll watch a lion dance with my family. And we'll visit my aunt and uncle. We'll have a big meal together. What festivals do you celebrate?

Yours,
Li Na

This is HARD WORK, but it'll bring GOOD LUCK.

sections.title: 第 78 页
sections.text:
MERRY CHRISTMAS

To: LiNa
Subject: Christmas

Dear Li Na,
The Spring Festival sounds fun! In the UK, our main festival is Christmas. About one month before Christmas, we put up a Christmas tree at home. On Christmas Eve, I always put a stocking by my bed. The next morning, there are gifts in it! My grandparents come on Christmas Day. First, we open our gifts. Then, we eat a big meal.

Yours,
Katie

How do the children and their families celebrate festivals?

sections.title: 第 79 页
sections.text:

What different activities do Li Na and Katle do?

**Spring Festival**
- visit relatives
- make jiaozi

**Christmas**

- put a stocking by the bed

Read again and talk.
How will Li Na and her family celebrate the Spring Festival?
They'll clean their house, Li Na will...

Think: Who do you celebrate the Spring Festival with? What do you usually do?

sections.title: 第 80 页
sections.text:
**Grammar in Use**

Listen and chant.
It's ten o'clock in the morning.
I'm sitting on a high-speed train.
In one hour, I'll be at my grandma's.
I can't wait to see her again!
She'll have our dinner ready.
My wish is on the way!
I'll greet the New Year with my family.
And we'll have fun for the next few days!

**Learn**
In one hour, I will be at my grandma's.
It will be the Spring Festival next month.

**Look and Say**
Li Na and her classmates' plan for the New Year party

| Date       | Activity                          |
|------------|-----------------------------------|
| 29 Dec.   | think about party games           |
| 30 Dec.   | buy food and drinks               |
| 31 Dec.   | have the party                    |
| 1 Jan.    | New Year holiday                  |

Here's Li Na and her classmates' plan for the New Year party. They'll think about party games this afternoon. They'll ... tomorrow.

sections.text:

Look at the card with a paper rabbit lantern. It's for the Lantern Festival. People make lanterns at the Lantern Festival.

Think: What special activities do people do at different festivals?

sections.title: 第 83 页
sections.text:
New Year's Day around the world

It will be New Year's Day in two days. How will you celebrate it? Let's find out how people around the world celebrate the New Year. It is always fun and exciting!

**China**
In China, we celebrate New Year's Day with our family and friends. We relax and have fun. We hope to have a good year.

**USA**
In the USA, people watch fireworks on New Year's Eve. On New Year's Day, many people watch American football matches.

sections.title: 第 84 页
sections.text:
Scotland

In Scotland, people visit their family and friends. They bring a piece of coal for good luck.

Spain

In Spain, people wait for the clock to ring at midnight. They eat 12 grapes—one for each chime.

and friends

Think: What activities do people do in different countries on New Year's Day?

Think more about the Big Question

We do special activities. How do we celebrate festivals?

We make and enjoy traditional food. We visit relatives and have a big meal together.
